# Privacy & security

Omni is a vault. The threat model assumes a motivated attacker who gets physical access to the unlocked Android filesystem (ADB shell, rooted backup, forensic pull). Everything here is designed so that even in that case, the valuable data does not come out in cleartext.

## What's sensitive

| Item | Sensitivity |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Full card number (PAN) | High — payment instrument |
| Card CVV | High — payment instrument (persisted here by explicit user opt-in, overriding the usual PCI practice of never storing it) |
| Document number (PAN card, Aadhaar, DL) | High — legal identity |
| Card photo, ID photo | Medium — contains all of the above visually |
| Expiry, holder name, issuer, tag, last4 | Low — shown in UI, persisted in app store |
| Task / alarm / note titles | Low — shown in UI, persisted in app store |

## Where each lives

| Data | Storage | Encryption | Notes |
| ---- | ------- | ---------- | ----- |
| PAN | `expo-secure-store` under `card_<id>_pan` | Android Keystore (AES-256-GCM, hardware-backed on most modern devices) | Only read when the card is mounted on screen or about to be shared |
| CVV | `expo-secure-store` under `card_<id>_cvv` | same as PAN | |
| Document number | `expo-secure-store` under `doc_<id>_num` | same as PAN | |
| Card photo URI | Zustand store | chunked, Keystore-backed | The photo file itself lives in app-private storage |
| ID photos (front/back) | Filesystem under app-private dir | — | Deleted when the document is deleted |
| Everything else (tasks, alarms, notes, metadata) | Zustand store persisted via chunked `secure-storage` wrapper | Android Keystore | Same backend as the sensitive items, not AsyncStorage |

The `secure-storage` wrapper chunks values into ~1.8 KB pieces before writing, because Android's `SecureStore` caps each entry at 2 KB.

## App lock

On every cold start:
1. Splash screen is held by `SplashScreen.preventAutoHideAsync()` until fonts + auth resolve.
2. `AppLockGate` calls `expo-local-authentication` with the system biometric prompt.
3. User passes biometric OR device PIN. Only then do the children render.

On every background → foreground transition:
- If the backgrounded gap is ≥ 15 seconds, the gate re-locks and runs auth again.
- If the gap is < 15 seconds (e.g., swipe up to change song and come back), no re-auth.

No custom Omni PIN is stored anywhere. The lock is your phone's lock.

## OCR

ML Kit Text Recognition runs in-process on the device. The image URI is a local file: path; nothing is uploaded. CVV is never parsed — even if it's visible on the card, the OCR layer ignores it by construction.

## Cloud proxy

Optional. If you set `EXPO_PUBLIC_PROXY_URL`, the app will POST the voice transcript text to `/api/ai/generate` for better classification, daily brief generation, or search answers. It never sends:
- Images (card / ID)
- Card numbers, CVVs, document numbers
- Anything from the secure store

If the proxy is not set, everything works locally with rule-based classifiers.

## Screen capture

Not currently prevented. Anyone with the phone unlocked can screenshot a card. The biometric gate is the barrier; once past it, you have the device.

## Deletion

- **Delete card** → removes from Zustand + deletes `card_<id>_pan`, `card_<id>_cvv` from secure store.
- **Delete document** → removes from Zustand + deletes `doc_<id>_num` from secure store + deletes both photo files from disk.
- **Voice "delete all cards"** → same, applied across all matching items, always behind a confirm screen.
- **Uninstall** → Android clears the app sandbox including the secure store. No residue.
